subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Rotating Black Holes: Kerr Black Holes
description: A 60-second lesson on Rotating Black Holes: Kerr Black Holes.

hook.text: Most black holes spin. A rotating black hole drags spacetime around with it like a cosmic whirlpool.

buildup.text: Roy Kerr solved the equations for spinning black holes in 1963. The solution was far more complex than the static case.

discovery.text: Rotating black holes have an ergosphere—a region outside the event horizon where space itself is dragged along.

twist.text: You could extract energy from a spinning black hole by throwing objects into the ergosphere. Penrose proposed this in 1969.

climax.text: Most real black holes spin near their maximum rate. Matter falling in adds angular momentum over billions of years.

punchline.text: Spinning black holes drag space itself. Even standing still, you'd rotate.

quiz.question: What is unique about the ergosphere of a rotating black hole?
options: Space itself is dragged along, making it impossible to stay still
It is a region of zero gravity surrounding the black hole
Light travels faster inside it than outside
